task_completion.description: Load the complete 24-subunit biological assembly of human H-chain ferritin (PDB: 1FHA).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL viewer should display a large, spherical, hollow cage-like structure composed of many subunits, not just a single small monomeric chain. The object panel may indicate '1FHA' and multiple chains or a biological assembly.

task_completion.description: Color the subunits distinctly to highlight the architecture.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ferritin cage in the PyMOL viewer should have its individual subunits colored in different colors (e.g., using a 'color by chain' command or a spectrum), making the multi-subunit architecture clearly visible.

task_completion.description: Measure the inner and outer diameters of the ferritin cage.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dashed measurement lines with numbers (in Angstroms) should be visible in the PyMOL viewer, spanning across the hollow core (inner diameter) and across the entire exterior (outer diameter) of the cage.

task_completion.description: Identify the two highly conserved acidic residues (Asp and Glu) lining the 3-fold symmetry channels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen inspecting the sequence viewer, selecting residues near the pores, or displaying labels for specific residues (Asp131 and Glu134) in the PyMOL viewer.

task_completion.description: Save a publication-quality figure of the 24-mer cage.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A command like 'png ~/PyMOL_Data/images/ferritin_cage.png' in the PyMOL command line, or a file manager/terminal showing the newly created 'ferritin_cage.png' file in the specified directory.

task_completion.description: Write a report containing the 24-mer confirmation, measured diameters, and pore-lining residues.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of '~/PyMOL_Data/ferritin_analysis_report.txt'. The text must confirm it is a 24-mer, state the inner diameter (~80 A) and outer diameter (~120 A), and list the residues Asp131 and Glu134.

integrity.description: The agent actually used PyMOL to load and view the structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: The PyMOL GUI should be visible in the screenshots with the 1FHA structure loaded, rather than the agent just writing a script blindly without verifying the visual output.

integrity.description: The agent used PyMOL's measurement tools rather than guessing or hallucinating the values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Measurement objects (e.g., 'measure01') should appear in the PyMOL object panel, and dashed measurement lines should be visible on the structure.

integrity.description: The agent created the report based on its findings rather than copying a pre-existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing or echoing the specific measured values and identified residues into the text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct PDB ID is 1FHA. The full biological assembly is a 24-mer cage. The inner diameter should be approximately 80 Angstroms (acceptable range: 70.0 - 90.0 A). The outer diameter should be approximately 120 Angstroms (acceptable range: 110.0 - 130.0 A). The two pore-lining acidic residues at the 3-fold symmetry channels are Asp131 (Aspartate 131) and Glu134 (Glutamate 134). The figure must be saved to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/ferritin_cage.png and the report to ~/PyMOL_Data/ferritin_analysis_report.txt.
